zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Yorke於1500年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾納希比時，他的父親Robert Yorke30歲，母親Margaret Self31歲。他於1531年之前與Emma "Emily" Self在英國諾薩普頓史爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1559年5月9日之前在他的家鄉，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼哥吉拉 沙智 亞德。

nameMeanings.name: Yorke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: York
meanings: English: habitational name from the city of York in northern England. The surname is now widespread throughout England. Originally, the city bore the Latin name Eburacum, which is probably from a Brittonic name meaning ‘yew-tree place’. This was altered by folk etymology to Old English Eoforwīc (from the elements eofor ‘wild boar’ + wīc ‘specialized farmstead’). This name was taken over by Scandinavian settlers, who altered it back to opacity in the form Jórvík or Jórk (English York, which became finally settled as the placename in the 13th century). The surname has also been adopted by Jews as an Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames.
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of the East Slavic patronymic   Yurkovich   or its Croatian, Slovak, or Slovenian variants. Compare   Yurk  .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Yorke was born in 1500, in Naseby, Northamptonshire, England, his father, Robert Yorke, was 30 and his mother, Margaret Self, was 31. He married Emma "Emily" Self before 1531, in Northamptonshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died before 9 May 1559, in his hometown, and was buried in All Saints Churchyard, Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom.

ko.All Saints Churchyard, Naseby, Daventry: 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 미들레턴 천이 앨 세인츠 c후르c히아어드
ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Yorke(이)가 1500년 영국 노르섬프토실르 나세비에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Yorke(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Self(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1531년 이전 영국 노르섬프토실르에서 Emma "Emily" Self(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1559년 5월 9일 이전 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 미들레턴 천이 앨 세인츠 c후르c히아어드에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Yorke родился в 1500 г. в местности неисеби, нортамптоншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert Yorke было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Self, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Emma "Emily" Self перед 1531 г. в местности нортамптоншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер перед 9 май 1559 г. в его родном городе и был похоронен в местности алл сеинц чурчярд, миддлетон чэни, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания.
